items.title: Cooperative Multiplex GNN for High-Grade Glioma Survival Prediction From Preoperative Multi-Modal Radiomics-Based Brain Networks
items.description: Accurately and preoperatively predicting survival for high-grade gliomas (HGGs) is important for optimizing treatment strategies. items.title: Insights From Particle Simulations for Diffusion MRI Microstructure Studies
items.description: Diffusion MRI mostly involves quantification of how water diffuses in tissue, and its relationship with tissue microstructure. items.authors: Viktor Vegh, Qianqian Yang, Megan Farquhar, Thomas R. Barrick

items.title: GLEAM: A Multimodal Imaging Dataset and HAMM for Glaucoma Classification
items.description: Glaucoma is a leading cause of irreversible blindness worldwide, with asymptomatic early stages often delaying diagnosis and treatment. items.authors: Jiao Wang, Chi Liu, Yiying Zhang, Hongchen Luo, et al.

items.title: Deep Learning for Temporal Super-Resolution 4D Flow MRI
items.description: 4D Flow Magnetic Resonance Imaging (4D Flow MRI) is a non-invasive technique for volumetric, time-resolved blood flow quantification. items.title: MT-SAM: A Mamba-Transformer Enhanced SAM With Prior-Guided Prompting for Multi-Modal Prostate Cancer Delineation

items.title: Positional Prompts-Enhanced Brain–Heart–Gut Interactions for Mild Cognitive Impairment Diagnosis
items.description: Mild cognitive impairment (MCI) is the prodromal stage of dementia involving complex interactions between the brain and peripheral organs. items.title: Head Motion Correction Based on Pilot Tone Signals in MRI: A Referenceless Method
items.description: Motion remains a problem in clinical MRI, largely because all existing effective correction methods tend to come with constraints on pulse sequence parameters, expensive/bulky equipment, or extra steps in the workflow. items.title: Refine Then Fusion: Robust 3D Brain MRI Synthesis via Vision–Language Collaboration
items.description: Metadata-guided cross-modality 3D MRI synthesis aims to generate target-contrast volumes from source-modality data conditioned on clinically available metadata, which is important for enhancing clinical imaging flexibility. items.authors: Jinbao Wei, Gang Yang, Wei Wei, Aiping Liu, et al.

items.title: DSHARP: Deep Incompressible Motion Estimation With Sinusoidal-Transformed Harmonic Phase for Tagged MRI
items.description: Tagged magnetic resonance imaging (tMRI) is a valuable tool for visualizing and quantifying tissue deformation in vivo. Its use is often hampered, however, by tag fading, long computation times, and the challenge of ensuring diffeomorphic, incompressible motion fields.
